frameworks.name: grid coding
frameworks.one_line: periodic entorhinal firing fields as a general cortical representational substrate

frameworks.name: dynamical systems theory
frameworks.one_line: sequence generation modeled as trajectories synthesized from multiple coupled dynamical systems

frameworks.name: compositional cognitive computation
frameworks.one_line: novel cognitive maps built by composing reusable spatial/temporal primitives

frameworks.name: neural network architectures for compositionality
frameworks.one_line: ML systems leveraging grid-like codes for analogous compositional generalization

core_question: How can grid-coded representations in the entorhinal-hippocampal system be composed to support flexible, compositional cognitive computations across space and time?
key_assumptions: grid coding is a general principle extending beyond MEC to areas such as PFC
hippocampal place-cell sequence reactivations reflect traversals of cognitive maps
cognitive maps can be analyzed as trajectories within dynamical systems
manipulations: synthesis of multiple dynamical systems to produce compositional sequences
simulated experiments probing compositionality in the spatial domain
simulated experiments probing compositionality in the temporal domain
key_findings: grid coding generates a diversity of empirically observed hippocampal place-cell reactivation sequences
composing multiple dynamical systems reproduces compositional sequence generation in space
the same compositional mechanism reproduces compositional sequence generation in time
interpretations: grid-coded dynamical systems can be combined to support compositional cognitive computation
the model links entorhinal-hippocampal sequence generation to compositional techniques in machine learning
